textByHash.0ce5a0ec5ec3b7662f7a5f0e9b2865bf43614ef5: After I lost my brother, who died following Lord Iorwerth's orders, I realised he needed to be stopped before it was too late.
textByHash.1aa8902bd2191081f72ed04dbbd63dbb5d4a06b8: He became so obsessed with his cause, he forgot why we started following him in the first place. He became more extreme, more radical.
textByHash.25ad4e3da8936cbecb78af1bb5e5bdeea5a6c4de: Well I can't really go back to Lord Iorwerth now. He knows I betrayed him.
textByHash.2d0127ccdbff62814e9e5f82c5627e35f628ea7f: Those that follow him, they do so because they believe in him, believe in what he's doing. For a long time, I believed in him as well.
textByHash.33172a1344e2f3f2f3c7834bd2ccd11d0473273f: There is a traitor amongst your people. I know not who they are, but it wasn't this human as you feared. They told Lord Iorwerth how to find the village.
textByHash.36d81ff80738e70cced0d18c1ac5868caca62440: It's not good I'm afraid. The human here was not wrong to suspect a betrayal. Lord Iorwerth, he knows about Lletya.
textByHash.3af87f5c2c1ccdbb639be446de01d18f2ffd6a1b: I tried to advise him as best I could, but over time he started listening more to Essyllt and less to me.
textByHash.47543a36c0e77f75fabc60f138c70a2097873798: I hope so. But even now I find myself filled with doubt.
textByHash.4e5c8c2760b55e1a259343ad81ca1fc4d39671b7: Earlier today. Essyllt is preparing their forces as we speak.
textByHash.649d8d8e87a0e79452db9b5eed0b360916facfbd: I've made my decision and I intend to stick by it.
textByHash.678fe2df4af4f8d8ab4fda9eb6af8eeb3f9c7849: I wish things were as simple as good against evil, but that's rarely the case.
textByHash.95a691b2a1a99c846b613e0478d2d18a8a160ca3: With no ruler, Prifddinas fell into chaos. When Lord Iorwerth came to us and said he'd found a way to bring Seren back to us, you can understand why we turned to him.
textByHash.a974ec9d610031e144edb13041347ef3a222050d: After we lost Seren, Baxtorian was chosen to lead us. Rather than looking after the city of Prifddinas, he chose to expand to the east, leaving us behind.
textByHash.ba3cc263c277692939c5990f55065e5995c2a62a: That was my request. I feared that the other rebels may not be trusting of me. They could have imprisoned me or even killed me. It seemed safer to keep things secret.
textByHash.bcbe4a53383ad4bb28cfcd54b0ecd1e5060223d7: He's not evil you know, Lord Iorwerth. None of them are really, apart from Essyllt maybe.
textByHash.dea41203603a5d161bd3dea8e14c27ae461698fd: Those are my people coming for us. My brothers and sisters.
textByHash.ed5237a2631ea63490a6c127ffb4cc9832d57c4f: Sorry to interrupt, but I think you need to hear this.
